name: Foodborne pathogens (sprouts and greens)
description: High humidity crops can harbor pathogens.
mitigation_summary: Use potable water; follow seed sprouting hygiene guidance from health agencies.

name: Fungal diseases
description: Leaf, stem, fruit, or tuber diseases caused by plant-pathogenic fungi (e.g. blights, mildews, rots) favored by humidity, poor airflow, or infected debris.
mitigation_summary: Use resistant varieties where available; spacing and airflow; avoid overhead wetting late in day; sanitation; rotate crops; scout and remove infected tissue per extension IPM.

name: Green tubers (solanine)
description: Potatoes exposed to light increase glycoalkaloids.
mitigation_summary: Hill soil; store dark; discard green or sprouted portions per extension guidance.

name: Heavy metals in produce
description: Uptake from contaminated soil or water.
mitigation_summary: Test soil; use raised beds and clean media; wash produce; follow local health guidance.

name: Mycotoxins in stored staples
description: Fungal toxins in improperly dried or stored grain/tuber.
mitigation_summary: Dry to safe moisture; cool dry airtight storage; inspect and discard suspect lots.

name: Pests
description: Insects, mites, nematodes, or other animals that damage plants or spread disease.
mitigation_summary: Monitor regularly; physical barriers and row cover; encourage beneficials; targeted controls per local extension integrated pest management.

name: Root rot / overwatering
description: Oxygen-starved roots and opportunistic rot organisms from saturated media or poor drainage.
mitigation_summary: Well-drained mix; containers with drainage; water when appropriate for species and season; avoid standing water in saucers.
